subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Stonehenge and the Sun
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Stonehenge and the Sun.

hook.text: Stonehenge's stones align perfectly with the sunrise on the summer solstice. It was built 5,000 years ago.

buildup.text: Ancient Britons moved 25-ton stones over 200 km to construct it. The effort suggests deep astronomical purpose.

discovery.text: The alignment tracks the Sun's extreme positions on the horizon—solstices and possibly lunar cycles too.

twist.text: Stonehenge may have been a calendar, a temple, or both. After 5,000 years, we still debate its exact purpose.

climax.text: Similar stone alignments exist worldwide—from Egypt to Peru. Ancient peoples everywhere watched the sky with precision.

punchline.text: Five thousand years old. Still aligned. The sky was humanity's first clock.

quiz.question: What astronomical event does Stonehenge align with?
options: The sunrise on the summer solstice
The position of the North Star
